discussion_posts.name: manhalfgod
discussion_posts.content:
If you also feel struggling to understand the ideas / intuition of LFU cache challenge,
try solving these 2 problems first:

1. [Maximum frequency stack](https://leetcode.com/problems/maximum-frequency-stack/)
2. [LRU cache](https://leetcode.com/problems/lru-cache/)

Learn how to track max (min in LFU challenge case) from the 1st question.
Learn the concept of ordered / sorted dictionary keys from the 2nd question.

Find your own favorite solutions for each question.

**Pro tip**: sort by most recent and learn from newer solution to take advantage of newer programming language features. Most voted solutions can be outdated / convoluted quickly.

For example, if you are using Python, since Python 3.7 a regular dictionary is guaranteed to keep keys insertion order; so you do not need to rely on OrderedDict.

discussion_posts.name: Jaltair

Great way to test if someone lies during the interview ))) 
Candidate (C), Interviewer (I) at Amazon. 
I: asks for LPs for part of the interview, uses the remaining 20-30 minutes to ask C to implement LFU
C: is able to come up and code optimal solution in 20 minutes
I: have you seen the problem before? 
C: no
I (silently) puts a red flag next to Earn Trust/Integrity ))))

discussion_posts.name: demaw
discussion_posts.content: after finally solving this question for  around a painstaking half hour full of troubleshooting and little adjustments, I read the problem description again.\nI missed the fact that all operations must be in O(1) time. I used a priority queue solution and both operations run in O(logn) time.\nNow I have to redo the entire problem...
